title: Count Equal Groups
essential_question: How can you find how many in all when you have equal groups?
strategies: Count: groups × items in each group

pretest.question: There are 3 bags. Each bag has 4 apples. How many apples in all?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104

learn.title: What Are Equal Groups?
learn.content: Equal groups all have the SAME number of objects. 3 plates with 4 cookies each = 3 equal groups of 4. If one plate has 5 cookies and the others have 4, the groups are NOT equal.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Skip Counting to Find the Total
learn.content: Skip count by the number in each group. For 4 groups of 5: count 5, 10, 15, 20. That's the total — 20 in all.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Groups Of
learn.content: '3 groups of 6' means 3 groups, each with 6 objects. Total = 6 + 6 + 6 = 18. This is the same as 3 × 6 = 18.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Repeated Addition
learn.content: Equal groups are the same as adding the same number over and over. 5 groups of 3 = 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 15. This idea is the start of multiplication.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Reading Problems Carefully
learn.content: Look for words like 'each', 'every', 'per' — they tell you equal groups. 'Each box has 4 pencils, 5 boxes' → 5 groups of 4, not 4 groups of 5. The number of GROUPS is 5; the SIZE of each group is 4.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 건너뛰며 세기 — 올바른 방향
learn.content: 곱셈에서 건너뛰며 세기는 '한 그룹의 크기'만큼 점프하고, '그룹의 개수'만큼 점프 횟수를 만듭니다. 예: 4 groups of 5 → 5씩 4번 점프 → 5, 10, 15, 20. 주의: 4씩 5번(4, 8, 12, 16, 20)으로 세면 답은 같아도 '몇 그룹?'을 물었을 때 틀린 모델이 됩니다. 팁: 손가락 하나가 한 그룹 — 손가락마다 점프 한 번.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: GROUPS와 SIZE 구별하기
learn.content: 문제를 읽을 때 '몇 개의 그룹(GROUPS)'과 '한 그룹에 몇 개(SIZE)'를 분리합니다. 예: '5개의 상자에 연필이 4개씩' → GROUPS=5, SIZE=4. '4개의 상자에 연필이 5개씩'과는 모델이 완전히 다름! 팁: GROUPS 단어에 밑줄, SIZE 단어에 동그라미. 'each / every / per' 뒤의 숫자 = SIZE.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 등량 그룹 풀이 4가지 전략 요약
learn.content: 전략 1 그리기: 각 그룹을 동그라미로 그리고 안에 점 찍기. 전략 2 건너뛰며 세기: SIZE만큼 점프, GROUPS만큼 횟수. 전략 3 반복 덧셈: SIZE를 GROUPS번 더하기. 전략 4 곱셈: GROUPS × SIZE = 합계. 검증: '같은 수 × 같은 수'가 되는지(등량 조건) 먼저 확인!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.1 'Count Equal Groups' pp.101-104
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic A — Multiplication and the Meaning of the Factors
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

choices: C. 2 boxes with 3 pencils, 1 box with 6
try.explanation: Only B has the same number (4) in every box.
hints: Equal groups have the SAME number in each.

feedback.incorrect: Equal groups must all have the same count.

practice_r2.question: Which picture shows 4 groups of 5?
choices: D. 4 bags with different coin counts
practice_r2.explanation: 4 groups of 5 = 4 bags, each bag has 5. The number of groups comes first.
hints: '4 groups of 5' — first number is GROUPS.
Second number is items PER group.

feedback.incorrect: 4 groups of 5 means 4 bags with 5 each.
